pi_items.verified_value: Latency = MSH-7 minus EVN-2, in minutes
pi_items.source: task.json description: 'Calculate latency in minutes by comparing the Message Date/Time (MSH-7) and the Recorded Date/Time (EVN-2). Latency = (MSH-7) - (EVN-2).'

pi_items.verified_value: MSH-7 at current time, EVN-2 at 20 minutes ago, latency = 20 minutes > 10 threshold
pi_items.source: export_result.sh lines 14-15,21 define NOW_TS and HIGH_LATENCY_TS (20 minutes ago)

pi_items.verified_value: MSH-7 at current time, EVN-2 at 2 minutes ago, latency = 2 minutes ≤ 10 threshold
pi_items.source: export_result.sh lines 16-17,24 define NOW_TS and NORMAL_LATENCY_TS (2 minutes ago)

task.json description, task_utils.sh, NextGen Connect documentation

privileged_info_summary: The HL7 Latency Monitoring Router task is a synthetic benchmark requiring creation of a NextGen Connect channel named 'Latency_Monitor' listening on TCP port 6661. The channel routes ADT^A01 HL7 messages based on latency calculated as the difference between MSH-7 (Message Date/Time) and EVN-2 (Recorded Date/Time). Messages with latency > 10 minutes route to /home/ga/latency_high/, and messages with latency ≤ 10 minutes route to /home/ga/latency_normal/. Timestamps are in yyyyMMddHHmmss format. Test data consists of synthetic messages with fabricated patient identifiers generated at task execution time (current time for MSH-7, 20 minutes ago and 2 minutes ago for EVN-2 in high and normal latency test cases respectively). Configuration and verification are performed via REST API at https://localhost:8443/api using HTTP Basic Authentication (admin:admin).
